items.title: Confusion Matrix
items.description: Each ROC point comes from TP, FP, TN, FN at one threshold. Confusion Matrix owns the 2×2 layout and cell definitions; this topic sweeps thresholds and plots TPR vs FPR derived from those counts.

items.title: Precision/Recall/F1
items.description: Precision, recall, and F1 are typically computed at one chosen threshold. ROC-AUC is threshold-free and uses TPR (recall) against FPR instead of precision. For rare positives, PR curves often pair with this topic — see Precision/Recall/F1 for single-threshold and PR-AUC context.

items.title: Logistic Regression
items.description: Logistic regression outputs P(y=1|x) via sigmoid — natural scores for ROC and AUC. Logistic Regression owns the sigmoid + BCE training; this topic consumes predict_proba scores for threshold-independent evaluation.

items.title: Support Vector Machines
items.description: SVMs expose decision_function margins (or Platt-scaled probabilities) that rank examples for ROC. Support Vector Machines owns the margin classifier; ROC-AUC judges how well those scores separate classes before picking a cutoff.

items.title: Train/Val/Test Splits
items.description: AUC must be computed on held-out labeled data — never on training scores used to fit the model. Train/Val/Test Splits defines leakage-safe evaluation protocol; report ROC-AUC on validation for model selection and on test once.
